senses.definition: someone who is trying to learn new things or become better at doing something
a person who is studying at a school, university, or college

examples.x: Can you introduce yourself to the new student in class?
She studies diligently to prepare for her exams as a student.
She takes notes during lectures to review later as a dedicated student.
The student raises their hand to answer the teacher's question.
They collaborate with other students on group projects.

senses.definition: a person who is being trained for a particular job or profession

examples.x: The company hired five new trainees this month.
As a trainee, she attended several training sessions.
The doctor supervised the medical trainee during surgery.
The trainee shadowed an experienced worker to learn the job.
He completed his trainee program and became a full-time employee.

senses.definition: a student, typically in a school or educational setting, who is learning under the guidance of a teacher

examples.x: The teacher instructed the pupils to complete their assignments before the end of class.
The school organized a field trip to enrich the pupils' understanding of science concepts.
Parents attended the parent-teacher meeting to discuss their child's progress as a pupil.
The pupil eagerly raised their hand to answer the teacher's question.
The school provided resources to support pupils with special educational needs.

senses.definition: someone who has a lot of knowledge about a particular subject, especially in the humanities

examples.x: The conference featured a renowned scholar who spoke about ancient Greek philosophy.
As a scholar of medieval literature, she has published numerous articles and books on the subject.
He was recognized as a leading scholar in the field of Renaissance art history.
The scholar spent years studying historical texts to uncover new insights about the period.
She is a respected scholar whose research has significantly contributed to our understanding of classical languages.

senses.definition: a student or trainee, especially one in a military academy or a program preparing for a career in the armed forces

examples.x: The cadet showed great discipline and determination during the rigorous training at the military academy.
Cadets undergo extensive physical and academic training to prepare them for future leadership roles in the armed forces.
As a cadet, he was expected to uphold the values of honor, integrity, and loyalty at all times.
Cadets participate in various drills and exercises to develop their skills in teamwork, strategy, and combat readiness.
Upon graduation, cadets are commissioned as officers and begin their service to their country in various branches of the military.

senses.definition: a student who resides and receives lodging within the school premises during the academic term

examples.x: Boarders at the school enjoy structured study sessions in the evenings.
She became a boarder at the prestigious institution to pursue her passion for music.
The school offers various recreational activities for boarders during weekends.
Boarders often form close bonds with their peers and housemasters.
Being a boarder provides students with a unique opportunity for personal growth and independence.

senses.definition: a student who is learning English as an additional language, often in a setting where English is the primary language of instruction
a student who temporarily attends a school or university in another country as part of an exchange program

examples.x: Lisa, an exchange student from Germany, is studying at our school for the semester.
As an exchange student, Javier had the opportunity to live with a host family and experience daily life in Japan.
The school organized a welcome party to greet the exchange students arriving from France, Spain, and Italy.
Being an exchange student broadened Sarah's perspective and gave her lifelong friendships from around the world.
Julia decided to become an exchange student to experience life in a new country and broaden her horizons.

senses.definition: an individual who has been elected or appointed to membership based on their achievements and contributions to the field

examples.x: She was honored to be elected as a fellow of the Royal Society for her groundbreaking research in physics.
As a fellow of the American Psychological Association, he regularly contributes to the advancement of psychological science.
The conference brought together fellows from around the world to exchange ideas and collaborate on research projects.
The institution grants funding and resources to fellows to support their research endeavors.
Being recognized as a fellow of the society is a prestigious achievement in the academic community.

senses.definition: an individual who pursues higher education later in life, often after a significant gap since completing secondary education

examples.x: After working in the industry for over a decade, Mark decided to enroll in university as a mature student to pursue a degree in computer science.
As a mature student, Sarah brings valuable life experience to the classroom discussions, enriching the learning environment for her peers.
The university offers flexible scheduling options to accommodate the diverse needs of mature students who may be balancing work, family, and studies.
Javier, a retired military veteran, returned to school as a mature student to fulfill his lifelong dream of studying history and becoming a teacher.
The support services for mature students include academic advising, career counseling, and workshops tailored to their specific needs and challenges.

senses.definition: someone who is not present at school, work, etc. when they are supposed to be
a person who is taking an examination or test to evaluate their knowledge, skills, or abilities

examples.x: The examinee completed the standardized test within the allotted time frame.
The examinee carefully reviewed their notes before the final exam.
The testing center provided accommodations for examinees with disabilities.

senses.definition: someone seeking admission to a program or being considered for an academic position, such as a scholarship or degree

examples.x: The university received numerous applications from qualified candidates for the scholarship program.
As a candidate for the doctoral program, Maria submitted her research proposal and academic transcripts for consideration.
The hiring committee interviewed several candidates for the faculty position before selecting the most qualified applicant.
After completing the required coursework, John became a candidate for graduation with a bachelor's degree in engineering.
The school board evaluated multiple candidates for the principal position and ultimately appointed Dr. Rodriguez based on her leadership experience and vision.

senses.definition: a graduate or undergraduate student who assists a professor or instructor in various tasks related to teaching

examples.x: The teaching assistant conducted review sessions to help students prepare for the upcoming exam.
During lab sessions, the teaching assistant provided guidance and assistance to students as they conducted experiments.
The teaching assistant graded homework assignments and provided feedback to students to help them improve their understanding of the material.
In the discussion sections, the teaching assistant facilitated engaging conversations and answered questions to clarify complex topics.
As a teaching assistant, she held office hours to provide one-on-one support to students who needed extra help with the course material.

senses.definition: a student leader who resides in a college dormitory and assists fellow students with various needs, such as resolving conflicts and enforcing rules

examples.x: As a resident assistant, Sarah helps organize events and provides support to students living in her dorm.
The resident assistant on duty responded promptly to noise complaints and ensured that quiet hours were observed.
Juan, a resident assistant, conducts regular check-ins with students to address any concerns and promote a positive living environment.
The resident assistant facilitated a floor meeting to discuss upcoming events and address any issues raised by residents.
She decided to apply to be a resident assistant because she enjoys helping others and fostering a sense of community in her dormitory.

senses.definition: a student who is in the year before the last year of college or high school

examples.x: As juniors, they are preparing for standardized tests and college entrance exams.
Mark is excited to be a junior this year and looking forward to senior year activities.
Sarah's brother is a junior at the local high school, participating in sports and clubs.
The juniors are working on their final projects before becoming seniors.
The juniors organized a farewell party for the graduating seniors.

senses.definition: a student who has one more year to graduate from a university or high school

examples.x: In college, seniors have the opportunity to complete a senior thesis.
The seniors are preparing for graduation and college.

senses.definition: a person who has been officially enrolled as a student in a college or university

examples.x: The university welcomed the new matriculates during freshman orientation.
Matriculates attended a series of introductory lectures to familiarize themselves with campus life.
The registrar's office processed the paperwork for incoming matriculates.
Matriculates received their student ID cards upon completing the registration process.
Orientation leaders led campus tours for the incoming matriculates to help them navigate the university.

senses.definition: a high-school or university student who is in their first year of education

examples.x: As a freshman, it's essential to seek guidance from seniors and professors for academic success.
Emily felt a mix of excitement and nervousness on her first day as a freshman.
John joined a club to get involved in campus life as a freshman.
Mark made new friends during his first week as a freshman in high school.
Sarah's brother is a freshman at the local university, studying computer science.

senses.definition: a student at a high school or university in their second year of education

examples.x: As a sophomore, I am taking more advanced classes in my major.
As a sophomore, it's essential to balance academics and extracurricular activities.
Her sister is a sophomore in high school and enjoys being part of the drama club.
Mark's brother is a sophomore in high school and has joined the debate team.
Sarah felt more confident as a sophomore and took on leadership roles.

senses.definition: a student who is in a lower academic year or level compared to others within the same educational institution

examples.x: Underclassmen often seek guidance from upperclassmen regarding course selection and extracurricular activities.
The school counselor provided support and resources to underclassmen transitioning to high school.
Upperclassmen mentors were assigned to help underclassmen adjust to college life.
The school held an orientation session specifically for incoming underclassmen to introduce them to campus resources and policies.

senses.definition: a person who has completed the requirements for a degree from a university or college and has been awarded it
a student who is trying to complete their first degree in college or university

examples.x: Undergraduates typically pursue bachelor's degrees before considering graduate studies.
Many undergraduates take advantage of research opportunities to deepen their understanding of their field of study.

senses.definition: a graduate student who is studying at a university to get a more advanced degree

examples.x: She decided to continue her education as a postgraduate to earn a master's degree in psychology.
The university offers numerous programs for postgraduate students seeking advanced qualifications.
He spent several years as a postgraduate researching renewable energy solutions.
The postgraduate community at the university is vibrant, with students from all over the world.
As a postgraduate, she had access to additional resources and mentorship opportunities.

senses.definition: someone who has completed a bachelor's degree, an undergraduate academic credential typically earned after three to four years of study
an elite student with the highest grade throughout school that gets chosen to give a speech at their graduation ceremony

examples.x: The valedictorian delivered an inspiring speech at the commencement ceremony, reflecting on the journey of the graduating class.
The valedictorian's speech resonated with her classmates, who applauded her accomplishments and words of wisdom.

senses.definition: the student who ranks second highest academically in their graduating class and often delivers a speech at the commencement ceremony

examples.x: The salutatorian's speech highlighted the values of perseverance and resilience that helped them achieve academic success.

senses.definition: a former female student or pupil of a school, university, or college

examples.x: The university honored Jane Doe as an exceptional alumna for her achievements in the field of engineering.
As an alumna of Yale University, she remains actively engaged in mentoring programs for current students.
She returned to campus as a guest speaker, inspiring current students with her experiences as a successful alumna.

senses.definition: a person, particularly a male one, who is a former student of a college, university, or school

examples.x: The university recognized John Smith as an outstanding alumnus for his contributions to the field of medicine.
As an alumnus of Harvard University, she remains actively involved in alumni events and fundraising efforts.
The alumni association organizes networking opportunities and social events to keep alumnus connected with their alma mater.
After graduation, he became a successful entrepreneur, making his alma mater proud of their accomplished alumnus.
The university's newsletter features stories about notable alumni, celebrating their achievements and contributions to society.

senses.definition: a person who has recently completed their education at a school and is transitioning to the next phase of their life

examples.x: The career fair aimed to provide guidance and support for school-leavers as they explored their options after graduation.
As a school-leaver, she faced the exciting challenge of deciding between starting a job or continuing her studies at university.
The government introduced initiatives to support school-leavers in gaining valuable skills and training for the workforce.
The school-leaver program offered internships and apprenticeships to help students transition smoothly into their chosen career paths.
He attended workshops and seminars designed to equip school-leavers with the necessary tools for success in the job market.

senses.definition: a person who possesses a wide range of knowledge and expertise across various disciplines

examples.x: Leonardo da Vinci is often regarded as the quintessential polymath due to his proficiency in art, science, engineering, and other areas.
As a polymath, she excelled in both literature and mathematics, showcasing her diverse talents.
The Renaissance period produced many polymaths who made significant contributions to multiple fields of knowledge.
His reputation as a polymath earned him admiration and respect from his peers.
Today, the term polymath is used to describe individuals who demonstrate exceptional versatility and mastery in numerous domains.

senses.definition: a child who attends classes at a school

examples.x: The schoolchild carried a backpack full of books.
Every schoolchild in the area participates in the annual sports day.
His younger sister is still a schoolchild, so she can't stay up too late.
In the park, a schoolchild was reading a book while others played.

senses.definition: a child or young person who attends school, typically in primary or secondary education
someone who is or was in the same class as you at school or college

examples.x: At the class reunion, old classmates reminisced about their shared experiences and achievements.
She reached out to her former classmates on social media to reconnect and catch up.
She became close friends with her classmate after working on a group project together.
He often studied with his classmates in the library during exam week.
The teacher encouraged collaboration among classmates to foster a supportive learning community.

senses.definition: a person who loves reading books and often spends a lot of time reading

examples.x: She’s a real bookworm who finishes a novel every week.
The library was a perfect place for a bookworm like him.
As a child, he was a quiet bookworm who preferred reading to playing outside.
The bookworm stayed up all night finishing her favorite series.
The bookworm spent hours browsing the bookstore.

senses.definition: a student who is often perceived as socially awkward or overly studious, and may be subject to ridicule by their peers

examples.x: He was often teased by his classmates for being a nerd, mocked for his thick glasses and bookish demeanor.
The group of popular kids laughed at the nerds, dismissing them as boring and uninteresting.
Despite his efforts to fit in, he couldn't shake off the label of being a nerd, ostracized for his love of studying and lack of interest in partying.
The movie portrayed the nerds as socially inept misfits who struggled to navigate high school life.
She felt like an outsider in school, labeled as a nerd by her peers and longing for acceptance from the popular crowd.

senses.definition: a person who overly emphasizes minor details or rules, often displaying a pretentious or excessive concern for academic correctness

examples.x: The pedant corrected everyone's grammar in class.
Don't be such a pedant about punctuation.
He's such a pedant when it comes to citing sources.
Being a pedant can sometimes alienate others.
The pedant couldn't resist pointing out the smallest errors.

senses.definition: a person, typically a child, who demonstrates exceptional talent or ability in a particular area, often beyond what is considered normal for their age

examples.x: The young pianist was a musical prodigy, performing at concerts by age six.
Scientists called her a prodigy after she solved complex equations as a teenager.
The chess prodigy defeated experienced grandmasters before turning ten.
His parents recognized he was a prodigy when he started composing music at five.

senses.definition: very passionate about studying or reading

examples.x: The studious student spent hours in the library each day.
Her studious nature made her a top performer in her class.
The studious reader devoured books on a wide range of topics.
Their studious group met every weekend to discuss philosophy.
The studious child preferred reading to playing outside.
